subject: Geology
title: Intensity and the MMI Scale
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Intensity and the MMI Scale.

hook.text: Magnitude tells you the quake's power. Intensity tells you how bad the shaking felt where you were standing.

buildup.text: The Modified Mercalli Intensity scale rates shaking from I (not felt) to XII (total destruction) based on effects.

discovery.text: One earthquake has one magnitude but many intensities. Intensity decreases with distance from the epicenter.

twist.text: Soft soil amplifies shaking. Two places the same distance from a quake can experience very different intensities.

climax.text: Intensity maps show where damage is worst. They guide rescue efforts and reveal which areas need better building codes.

punchline.text: The same quake shakes every place differently.

quiz.question: What does the Modified Mercalli Intensity scale measure?
options: How strongly shaking is felt and its effects at a location
